CheckTitle: Secret Manager secrets have automatic rotation enabled within 90 days
Description: Ensures that all secrets stored in Google Cloud Secret Manager have automatic rotation configured with a rotation period of 90 days or less. Periodic rotation limits the exposure window of a compromised secret.
Risk: Without automatic rotation, a leaked or compromised secret remains valid indefinitely, increasing the blast radius of a credential exposure incident and violating the principle of least privilege over time.

Code.Other:
1. Go to the Google Cloud Console > Security > Secret Manager.
2. Select the secret.
3. Click Edit secret.
4. Under Rotation, enable Automatic rotation and set a rotation period of 90 days or less.
5. Click Save.

Recommendation.Text: Enable automatic rotation for all Secret Manager secrets with a period of 90 days or less. Use Pub/Sub notifications to trigger rotation logic, and ensure the secret version is updated at each rotation event.

Notes: A secret without a rotationPeriod field has no automatic rotation configured and will be marked as FAIL. Rotation periods exceeding 90 days are also marked as FAIL.
